name: Federal Law on the Protection of Personal Data Held by Private Parties (LFPDPPP)
summary: Mexico's reformed federal data protection law, published March 2025, replaces the original 2010 LFPDPPP. It maintains ARCO rights (Access, Rectification, Cancellation, Opposition), incorporates data minimization and proactive accountability, and transfers enforcement from the dissolved INAI to the Secretariat of Anti-Corruption and Good Governance.
keyRequirements: Provide a comprehensive privacy notice (aviso de privacidad) before processing
Obtain consent (express consent required for sensitive and financial data)
Respect ARCO rights (Access, Rectification, Cancellation, Opposition)
Implement administrative, technical, and physical security measures
Execute data processing agreements with data processors
Restrict cross-border transfers to adequate jurisdictions or with consent
Designate a person or department responsible for data protection
penalties: Administrative fines range from 100 to 320,000 UMA. Criminal sanctions of 3 months to 5 years imprisonment for security breaches and deceitful processing. Enforced by the Secretariat of Anti-Corruption and Good Governance.
